```{r}
plot(livestock_classified_10)

# Step 3: Encode states
livestock_M <- ifel(M_sub == 1 & P_CN_UA_threshold == 0, livestock_classified_10 + 20, NA)
livestock_P <- ifel(P_CN_UA_threshold == 1 & M_sub == 0, livestock_classified_10 + 10, NA)
livestock_none <- ifel(M_sub == 0 & P_CN_UA_threshold == 0, livestock_classified_10, NA)

# Step 4: Merge the rasters
livestock_combined <- merge(livestock_none, livestock_P, livestock_M)
plot(livestock_combined)

# Step 5: Convert to dataframe
livestock_df <- as.data.frame(livestock_combined, xy = TRUE)
colnames(livestock_df) <- c("x", "y", "value")

# Step 6: Define 30-class palette
abandon_palette <- c(
  # 1-10: Shades of grey for no abandonment
  "#FAFAFA", "#F0F0F0", "#E0E0E0", "#D6D6D6", "#C4C4C4",
  "#B0B0B0", "#9E9E9E", "#7D7D7D", "#616161", "#424242",
  # 11-20: Shades of purple for P_SUM species
  "#F3E5F5", "#E1BEE7", "#CE93D8", "#BA68C8", "#AB47BC",
  "#9C27B0", "#8E24AA", "#7B1FA2", "#6A1B9A", "#4A148C",
  # 21-30: Shades of brown for M_SUM species
  "#fff4e3", "#fee0b6", "#fdd697", "#fdb863", "#f29842",
  "#e08214", "#cc6d0d", "#b35806", "#a34906", "#7f3b08"
)

# Step 7: Assign colors
livestock_df <- livestock_df %>%
  mutate(color = case_when(
    value >= 1  & value <= 30 ~ abandon_palette[value],
    TRUE ~ "#d3d3d3"
  ))

# Step 8: Map
livestock_map <- ggplot() +
  geom_tile(data = livestock_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = color)) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(2635973, 6525973), ylim = c(1448659, 5415659), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )  +
  labs(
    title = "Wildlife expansion and low livestock density"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      size = 18,        # font size (increase/decrease as needed)
      face = "bold",    # makes it bold
      hjust = 0.5       # centers the title
    ))

# Step 9: Save

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'livestock_wolf_bear_species_map_260324.png'),
  plot = livestock_map,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

# LEGEND SECTION ------------------------------------------------------------

# Step 10: Build legend df
legend_df <- expand.grid(
  base_level = 1:10,
  expansion = c("No species gained", "P_SUM gained", "M_SUM gained")
)

# Step 11: Recode and assign palette
legend_palette <- abandon_palette
legend_df <- legend_df %>%
  mutate(
    expansion = recode(
      expansion,
      "No species gained" = "Species absent",
      "P_SUM gained" = "Species gained (proba > 0.5)",
      "M_SUM gained" = "No new species"
    ),
    color = case_when(
      expansion == "Species absent" ~ legend_palette[base_level],
      expansion == "Species gained (proba > 0.5)" ~ legend_palette[base_level + 10],
      expansion == "No new species" ~ legend_palette[base_level + 20]
    )
  )

livestock_legend_limits <- round(livestock_breaks)

# Step 12: Plot legend
livestock_legend <- ggplot(legend_df, aes(x = base_level, y = expansion, fill = color)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(0.5, 10.5, 1),
    labels = livestock_legend_limits,
    position = 'top'
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Livestock (count/km2)",
    y = "Species expansion status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 14, angle = 0, vjust = 1.6, hjust = 1, margin = margin(r = -80)),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, angle = 90),
    axis.text.x.top = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0),
    axis.title.x.top = element_text(size = 14, margin = margin(b = 20)),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 100)
  ) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.5)

# Step 13: Display
plot(livestock_legend)

# Step 14: Save
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'livestock_wolf_bear_species_legend_260324.png'),
  plot = livestock_legend,
  width = 24, height = 10, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

# The figure in the article is built upon the maps and legend as separate .png files, here are two supplementary steps to have both together

############ Combine map and legend

# Combine the fixed legend with the map
final_plot <- plot_grid(livestock_map, livestock_legend, ncol = 2)

# Save combined
ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "livestock_wolf_bear_species_map_with_legend_260324.png"),
  plot = final_plot,
  width = 40, height = 25, units = "cm", dpi = 300
)

```

## IV. Vizualising overlap of the four benefits (top 80% of each)

# 1. Determining when benefits overlap

Get the top 80% of each benefits

```{r}

# Function to compute binary raster for top (80%) bins x binary mask, preserving NA
extract_top80_bins <- function(classified_raster, binary_mask, top_bins = c(3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10)) {
  result <- ifel(is.na(classified_raster) | is.na(binary_mask), NA,
                 ifel(classified_raster %in% top_bins & binary_mask == 1, 1, 0))
  return(result)
}

# Apply function to each pair
carbon_top80_stack <- extract_top80_bins(pot_carbon_classified, abandonment_masked)
cv_top80_stack  <- extract_top80_bins(cv_classified, abandonment_masked)
wtt_top80_stack <- extract_top80_bins(WTT_classified, P_SUM_threshold)
livestock_top80_stack <- extract_top80_bins(livestock_classified_10, P_CN_UA_threshold)

# Stack for inspection or export
top80_stacks <- c(carbon_top80_stack, cv_top80_stack, wtt_top80_stack, livestock_top80_stack)

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(carbon_top80_stack)
plot(cv_top80_stack)
plot(wtt_top80_stack)
plot(livestock_top80_stack)

# Export to the correct output directory
writeRaster(top80_stacks, filename = file.path(output_dir, "rewilding_opportunity_top80_260324.tif"), overwrite = TRUE)

# NPP Top 80%
png(file.path(output_dir, "carbon_top80_map.png"), width = 800, height = 800, res = 150)
plot(carbon_top80_stack, main = "Top 80% NPP × Abandonment")
dev.off()

# CV Top 80%
png(file.path(output_dir, "cv_top80_map.png"), width = 800, height = 800, res = 150)
plot(cv_top80_stack, main = "Top 80% CV × Abandonment")
dev.off()

# WTT Top 80%
png(file.path(output_dir, "wtt_top80_map.png"), width = 800, height = 800, res = 150)
plot(wtt_top80_stack, main = "Top 80% WTT × Expansion")
dev.off()

# Livestock Top 80%
png(file.path(output_dir, "livestock_top80_map.png"), width = 800, height = 800, res = 150)
plot(livestock_top80_stack, main = "Top 80% Livestock × Expansion")
dev.off()

```

Determining when the four overlap and when they never overlap

```{r}

# Load the four binary top 80% raster layers
carbon80  <- carbon_top80_stack
cv80      <- cv_top80_stack
wtt80     <- wtt_top80_stack
livestock80 <- livestock_top80_stack

### When they never overlapp

# Function to isolate exclusive presence (1) while others are 0
only_one <- function(target, others) {
  result <- ifelse(target == 1 & rowSums(others) == 0, 1, 0)
  result[is.na(target) | apply(is.na(others), 1, any)] <- NA
  return(result)
}

# Convert to matrices for row-wise operations
# Combine other layers excluding each target
others_cv80 <- c(carbon80, wtt80, livestock80)
others_carbon80 <- c(cv80, wtt80, livestock80)
others_wtt80 <- c(carbon80, cv80, livestock80)
others_livestock80 <- c(carbon80, cv80, wtt80)

# Compute exclusive layers
carbon_only80 <- only_one(values(carbon80), values(others_carbon80))
cv_only80 <- only_one(values(cv80), values(others_cv80))
wtt_only80 <- only_one(values(wtt80), values(others_wtt80))
livestock_only80 <- only_one(values(livestock80), values(others_livestock80))

# Assign back to raster objects
carbon_only_r80 <- setValues(carbon80, carbon_only80)
cv_only_r80 <- setValues(cv80, cv_only80)
wtt_only_r80 <- setValues(wtt80, wtt_only80)
livestock_only_r80 <- setValues(livestock80, livestock_only80)

# Combine into a stack
exclusive_stack80 <- c(carbon_only_r80, cv_only_r80, wtt_only_r80, livestock_only_r80)
names(exclusive_stack80) <- c("carbon_only80", "cv_only80", "wtt_only80", "livestock_only80")

# Plot or export
plot(exclusive_stack80)
writeRaster(exclusive_stack80, filename = file.path(output_dir, "exclusive_overlap_stack80_260324.tif"), overwrite = TRUE)

```

No overlap

```{r}

plot(exclusive_stack80)
names(exclusive_stack80)

# Step 1: Assign unique values
carbon_map <- ifel(exclusive_stack80$carbon_only80 == 1, 1, NA)
cv_map <- ifel(exclusive_stack80$cv_only80 == 1, 2, NA)
wtt_map <- ifel(exclusive_stack80$wtt_only80 == 1, 3, NA)
livestock_map <- ifel(exclusive_stack80$livestock_only80 == 1, 4, NA)

# Add background category = 0 where all are NA
all_na <- is.na(carbon_map) & is.na(cv_map) & is.na(wtt_map) &
          is.na(livestock_map)

background_map <- ifel(all_na, 0, NA)  # gray background

# Step 2: Combine layers (priority-based, highest takes precedence)
combined_map <- merge(
  background_map,
  carbon_map, cv_map, wtt_map, livestock_map
)

combined_map_masked <- mask(combined_map, inland_raster)

# Step 3: Convert to dataframe for ggplot
map_df <- as.data.frame(combined_map_masked, xy = TRUE)
colnames(map_df)[3] <- "category"

# Remove NA rows only now
map_df <- map_df[!is.na(map_df$category), ]

# Step 4: Define custom color palette and labels
category_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#ECECEC",  # gray background
  "1" = "#FFD128",
  "2" = "#43C245",
  "3" = "#7DDEEE",
  "4" = "#DB7DEE"
)

category_labels <- c(
  "0" = "No opportunity",
  "1" = "High mitigation",
  "2" = "High adaptation",
  "3" = "High social benefit",
  "4" = "Low conflict"
)

# Saving for the later map
map_df_opp_single_80 <- map_df

# Step 5: Plot with ggplot + borders and coastlines
map_opportunities_one <- ggplot(map_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = factor(category))) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = category_colors,
    labels = category_labels,
    name = "Single rewilding opportunity"
  ) +
  # Add country borders and coastlines
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),  # adapte les limites si nécessaire
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'no_overlap_stack80_260324.png'),
  plot = map_opportunities_one,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

```

Determining when two benefits overlap

```{r}

### When two layers overlap

# Ensure NA-safe pairwise overlap
binary_overlap <- function(x, y, others) {
  # Condition: x == 1 & y == 1 & all others == 0
  overlap <- (x == 1) & (y == 1)
  exclude <- Reduce("+", lapply(others, function(z) z == 1))
  result <- ifel(overlap & (exclude == 0), 1, 0)
  result[is.na(x) | is.na(y) | Reduce("|", lapply(others, is.na))] <- NA
  return(result)
}

# Compute 2-way overlaps
carbon_cv80        <- binary_overlap(carbon80, cv80, list(wtt80, livestock80))
carbon_wtt80      <- binary_overlap(carbon80, wtt80, list(cv80, livestock80))
carbon_livestock80 <- binary_overlap(carbon80, livestock80, list(cv80, wtt80))
cv_wtt80        <- binary_overlap(cv80, wtt80, list(carbon80, livestock80))
cv_livestock80  <- binary_overlap(cv80, livestock80, list(carbon80, wtt80))
wtt_livestock80 <- binary_overlap(wtt80, livestock80, list(carbon80, cv80))

# Stack results
overlap_stack80 <- c(carbon_cv80, carbon_wtt80, carbon_livestock80, cv_wtt80, cv_livestock80, wtt_livestock80)
names(overlap_stack80) <- c("carbon_cv80", "carbon_wtt80", "carbon_livestock80", "cv_wtt80", "cv_livestock80", "wtt_livestock80")

# Plot or save
plot(overlap_stack80)

# Step 1: Assign unique values
carbon_cv_map80 <- ifel(overlap_stack80$carbon_cv80 == 1, 1, NA)
carbon_wtt_map80 <- ifel(overlap_stack80$carbon_wtt80 == 1, 2, NA)
carbon_livestock_map80 <- ifel(overlap_stack80$carbon_livestock80 == 1, 3, NA)
cv_wtt_map80 <- ifel(overlap_stack80$cv_wtt80 == 1, 4, NA)
cv_livestock_map80 <- ifel(overlap_stack80$cv_livestock80 == 1, 5, NA)
wtt_livestock_map80 <- ifel(overlap_stack80$wtt_livestock80 == 1, 6, NA)

# Add background category = 0 where all are NA
all_na <- is.na(carbon_cv_map80) & is.na(carbon_wtt_map80) & is.na(carbon_livestock_map80) &
          is.na(cv_wtt_map80) & is.na(cv_livestock_map80) & is.na(wtt_livestock_map80)

background_map <- ifel(all_na, 0, NA)  # gray background

# Step 2: Combine layers (priority-based, highest takes precedence)
combined_map <- merge(
  background_map,
  carbon_cv_map80, carbon_wtt_map80, carbon_livestock_map80, cv_wtt_map80, cv_livestock_map80, wtt_livestock_map80  
)

combined_map_masked <- mask(combined_map, inland_raster)

# Step 3: Convert to dataframe for ggplot
map_df <- as.data.frame(combined_map_masked, xy = TRUE)
colnames(map_df)[3] <- "category"

# Remove NA rows only now
map_df <- map_df[!is.na(map_df$category), ]

# Step 4: Define custom color palette and labels
category_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#ECECEC",  # gray background
  "1" = "#D47000",
  "2" = "#00FFE4",
  "3" = "#6C00FF",
  "4" = "#2AFF00",
  "5" = "#0059FF",
  "6" = "#EC01FF"
)

category_labels <- c(
  "0" = "No opportunity",
  "1" = "High mitigation & adaptation",
  "2" = "High mitigation & social benefit",
  "3" = "High mitigation & low conflict",
  "4" = "High adaptation & social benefit",
  "5" = "High adaptation & low conflict",
  "6" = "High social benefit & low conflict"
)

# Saving for the later map
map_df_opp_two_80 <- map_df

# Step 5: Plot with ggplot + borders and coastlines
map_opportunities_two <- ggplot(map_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = factor(category))) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = category_colors,
    labels = category_labels,
    name = "Two rewilding opportunities"
  ) +
  # Add country borders and coastlines
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),  # adapte les limites si nécessaire
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'two_overlap_stack80_260324.png'),
  plot = map_opportunities_two,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

```

Determining when three benefits overlap

```{r}

### When three layers overlap

# Function to compute 3-way overlap while the 4th is 0
three_way_overlap <- function(a, b, c, not_d) {
  combo <- (a == 1) & (b == 1) & (c == 1) & (not_d == 0)
  combo[is.na(a) | is.na(b) | is.na(c) | is.na(not_d)] <- NA
  return(combo)
}

# Compute all 4 possible 3-way overlaps (excluding one each time)
overlap_carbon_cv_wtt80     <- three_way_overlap(carbon80, cv80, wtt80, livestock80)
overlap_carbon_cv_livest80 <- three_way_overlap(carbon80, cv80, livestock80, wtt80)
overlap_carbon_wtt_livest80 <- three_way_overlap(carbon80, wtt80, livestock80, cv80)
overlap_cv_wtt_livest80  <- three_way_overlap(cv80, wtt80, livestock80, carbon80)

# Combine into a stack
overlap_3_stack80 <- c(
  overlap_carbon_cv_wtt80,
  overlap_carbon_cv_livest80,
  overlap_carbon_wtt_livest80,
  overlap_cv_wtt_livest80
)
names(overlap_3_stack80) <- c("carbon_cv_wtt80", "carbon_cv_livest80", "carbon_wtt_livest80", "cv_wtt_livest80")

plot(overlap_3_stack80)
names(overlap_3_stack80)

# Step 1: Assign unique values
carbon_cv_wtt_map80 <- ifel(overlap_3_stack80$carbon_cv_wtt80 == 1, 1, NA)
carbon_cv_livest_map80 <- ifel(overlap_3_stack80$carbon_cv_livest80 == 1, 2, NA)
carbon_wtt_livest_map80 <- ifel(overlap_3_stack80$carbon_wtt_livest80 == 1, 3, NA)
cv_wtt_livest_map80 <- ifel(overlap_3_stack80$cv_wtt_livest80 == 1, 4, NA)

# Add background category = 0 where all are NA
all_na <- is.na(carbon_cv_wtt_map80) & is.na(carbon_cv_livest_map80) & is.na(carbon_wtt_livest_map80) & is.na(cv_wtt_livest_map80)

background_map <- ifel(all_na, 0, NA)  # gray background

# Step 2: Combine layers (priority-based, highest takes precedence)
combined_map <- merge(
  background_map,
  carbon_cv_wtt_map80, carbon_cv_livest_map80, carbon_wtt_livest_map80, cv_wtt_livest_map80
)

combined_map_masked <- mask(combined_map, inland_raster)

# Step 3: Convert to dataframe for ggplot
map_df <- as.data.frame(combined_map_masked, xy = TRUE)
colnames(map_df)[3] <- "category"

# Remove NA rows only now
map_df <- map_df[!is.na(map_df$category), ]

# Step 4: Define custom color palette and labels
category_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#ECECEC",  # gray background
  "1" = "#000FFF",
  "2" = "#00C112", 
  "3" = "#BA01FF",
  "4" = "#01FFEC"
)

category_labels <- c(
  "0" = "No opportunity",
  "1" = "High mitigation, high adaptation & high social benefit",
  "2" = "High mitigation, high adaptation & low conflict",
  "3" = "High mitigation, high social benefit & low conflict",
  "4" = "High adaptation, high social benefit & low conflict"
)

# Saving for the later map
map_df_opp_three_80 <- map_df 

# Step 5: Plot with ggplot + borders and coastlines
map_opportunities_three <- ggplot(map_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = factor(category))) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = category_colors,
    labels = category_labels,
    name = "Three rewilding opportunities"
  ) +
  # Add country borders and coastlines
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),  # adapte les limites si nécessaire
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'three_overlap_stack80_260324.png'),
  plot = map_opportunities_three,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

```

Determining when the four benefits overlap

```{r}

### When four layers overlap
combined_top80_all1 <- ifel(
  is.na(carbon80) | is.na(cv80) |
  is.na(wtt80) | is.na(livestock80),
  NA,
  ifel(carbon80 == 1 & cv80 == 1 &
       wtt80 == 1 & livestock80 == 1,
       1, 0)
)

plot(combined_top80_all1)
names(combined_top80_all1)

combined_top80_all1_masked <- mask(combined_top80_all1, inland_raster)

# Step 1: Convert to dataframe
map_df <- as.data.frame(combined_top80_all1_masked, xy = TRUE)
colnames(map_df)[3] <- "category"

# Remove NA rows only
map_df <- map_df[!is.na(map_df$category), ]

map_df <- map_df %>%
  filter(x >= 2635973, x <= 6525973, y >= 1448659, y <= 5415659)

# Step 2: Define color palette and labels
category_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#ECECEC",  # gray background
  "1" = "#01FF05"   # you can pick another color
)

category_labels <- c(
  "0" = "No opportunity",
  "1" = "High mitigation, high adaptation &\n high social benefit & low conflict"
)

# Saving for the later map
map_df_opp_four_80 <- map_df

# Step 3: Plot
map_opportunities_four <- ggplot(map_df, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = factor(category))) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = category_colors,
    labels = category_labels,
    name = "Four rewilding opportunities"
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'four_overlap_stack80_260324.png'),
  plot = map_opportunities_four,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

```

Map the number of overlaps

```{r}

# Step 1: Keep 0 when 0, retain all values
map_df_opp_single_80$category <- ifelse(map_df_opp_single_80$category != 0, 1, 0)
map_df_opp_two_80$category    <- ifelse(map_df_opp_two_80$category != 0, 2, 0)
map_df_opp_three_80$category  <- ifelse(map_df_opp_three_80$category != 0, 3, 0)
map_df_opp_four_80$category   <- ifelse(map_df_opp_four_80$category != 0, 4, 0)

# Step 2: Keep only rows where category != 0 (to avoid double-counting), retain ocean as NA
df1 <- map_df_opp_single_80[map_df_opp_single_80$category != 0, ]
df2 <- map_df_opp_two_80[map_df_opp_two_80$category != 0, ]
df3 <- map_df_opp_three_80[map_df_opp_three_80$category != 0, ]
df4 <- map_df_opp_four_80[map_df_opp_four_80$category != 0, ]

# Step 3: Merge priority-wise (4 → 3 → 2 → 1)
map_df <- rbind(df1, df2, df3, df4)
map_df <- map_df[order(map_df$x, map_df$y, -map_df$category), ]  # prioritize higher category
map_df <- map_df[!duplicated(map_df[, c("x", "y")]), ]            # remove duplicates, keep highest

# Step 4: Rebuild NA + 0 base from any of the original full maps
base_template <- map_df_opp_single_80[, c("x", "y")]
map_df_final <- merge(base_template, map_df, by = c("x", "y"), all.x = TRUE)

# Step 5: Replace NA in category with 0 (only for land areas that had no opportunities)
# Note: leave real NA (ocean) untouched
map_df_final$category <- ifelse(
  is.na(map_df_final$category) & !is.na(map_df_opp_single_80$category),
  0,
  map_df_final$category
)

# Step 6: Factor conversion for plotting
map_df_final$category <- factor(map_df_final$category, levels = 0:4)

# Step 4: Define custom color palette and labels
category_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#FFFFFF",  # white background
  "1" = "#A9BF00",  
  "2" = "#FCCA3F",  
  "3" = "#FC3F3F",  
  "4" = "#7363FF"   
)

category_labels <- c(
  "0" = "No opportunity",
  "1" = "Single opportunity",
  "2" = "Two opportunities",
  "3" = "Three opportunities",
  "4" = "Four opportunities"
)

# Step 5: Plot base map
map_opportunities_number <- ggplot(map_df_final, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = factor(category))) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = category_colors,
    labels = category_labels,
    name = "Rewilding potential"
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'number_of_overlap_stack80_260324.png'),
  plot = map_opportunities_number,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

```

# 2. Determining benefits extent: distinguishing overlapping extent and exclusive extent 

Climate change mitigation

```{r}

######## Carbon
# Whole extent
plot(carbon80)
# Extent alone
plot(exclusive_stack80$carbon_only80)

# Step 1: Reclassify rasters into 0/1
r_whole <- carbon80
r_excl  <- exclusive_stack80$carbon_only80

# Step 2: Combine with priority to exclusive
# Start with whole extent
r_combined <- ifel(r_whole == 1, 1, 0)  # 1 = whole
# Overwrite with exclusive where present
r_combined <- ifel(r_excl == 1, 2, r_combined)  # 2 = exclusive

# Step 3: Convert to data frame for ggplot
df_combined <- as.data.frame(r_combined, xy = TRUE)
names(df_combined)[3] <- "category"
df_combined$category <- factor(df_combined$category, levels = c(0, 1, 2))

# Step 4: Define colors and labels
combined_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#FFFFFF",   # white
  "1" = "#0090FF",   # light blue = whole extent
  "2" = "#D10000"    # dark blue = exclusive
)

combined_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Overlapping extent",
  "2" = "Exclusive extent"
)

# Step 5: Plot
map_combined_carbon <- ggplot(df_combined, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = category)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = combined_colors,
    breaks = c("1", "2"),    # show only meaningful classes
    labels = combined_labels[c("1", "2")],
    name = "CC mitigation opportunity"
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(title = "Climate change mitigation opportunity (top 80%)")

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'opportunities80_mitigation_260327.png'),
  plot = map_combined_carbon,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

```

Climate change adaptation

```{r}

# Whole extent
plot(cv_top80_stack)
# Extent alone
plot(exclusive_stack80$cv_only80)

# Step 1: Reclassify rasters into 0/1
r_whole <- cv_top80_stack
r_excl  <- exclusive_stack80$cv_only80

# Step 2: Combine with priority to exclusive
# Start with whole extent
r_combined <- ifel(r_whole == 1, 1, 0)  # 1 = whole
# Overwrite with exclusive where present
r_combined <- ifel(r_excl == 1, 2, r_combined)  # 2 = exclusive

# Step 3: Convert to data frame for ggplot
df_combined <- as.data.frame(r_combined, xy = TRUE)
names(df_combined)[3] <- "category"
df_combined$category <- factor(df_combined$category, levels = c(0, 1, 2))

# Step 4: Define colors and labels
combined_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#FFFFFF",   # white
  "1" = "#0090FF",   # light blue = whole extent
  "2" = "#D10000"    # dark blue = exclusive
)

combined_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Overlapping extent",
  "2" = "Exclusive extent"
)

# Step 5: Plot
map_combined_cv <- ggplot(df_combined, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = category)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = combined_colors,
    breaks = c("1", "2"),    # show only meaningful classes
    labels = combined_labels[c("1", "2")],
    name = "CC adaptation opportunity"
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(title = "Climate change adaptation opportunity (top 80%)")

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'opportunities80_adaptation_260327.png'),
  plot = map_combined_cv,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

```

Social benefits

```{r}

# Whole extent
plot(wtt_top80_stack)
# Extent alone
plot(exclusive_stack80$wtt_only80)

# Step 1: Reclassify rasters into 0/1
r_whole <- wtt_top80_stack
r_excl  <- exclusive_stack80$wtt_only80

# Step 2: Combine with priority to exclusive
# Start with whole extent
r_combined <- ifel(r_whole == 1, 1, 0)  # 1 = whole
# Overwrite with exclusive where present
r_combined <- ifel(r_excl == 1, 2, r_combined)  # 2 = exclusive

# Step 3: Convert to data frame for ggplot
df_combined <- as.data.frame(r_combined, xy = TRUE)
names(df_combined)[3] <- "category"
df_combined$category <- factor(df_combined$category, levels = c(0, 1, 2))

# Step 4: Define colors and labels
combined_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#FFFFFF",   # white
  "1" = "#0090FF",   # light blue = whole extent
  "2" = "#D10000"    # dark blue = exclusive
)

combined_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Overlapping extent",
  "2" = "Exclusive extent"
)

# Step 5: Plot
map_combined_wtt <- ggplot(df_combined, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = category)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = combined_colors,
    breaks = c("1", "2"),    # show only meaningful classes
    labels = combined_labels[c("1", "2")],
    name = "Ecotourism opportunity"
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(title = "Ecotourism opportunity (top 80%)")

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'opportunities80_wtt_260327.png'),
  plot = map_combined_wtt,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)


```

Sociale conflicts

```{r}

# Whole extent
plot(livestock_top80_stack)
# Extent alone
plot(exclusive_stack80$livestock_only80)

# Step 1: Reclassify rasters into 0/1
r_whole <- livestock_top80_stack
r_excl  <- exclusive_stack80$livestock_only80

# Step 2: Combine with priority to exclusive
# Start with whole extent
r_combined <- ifel(r_whole == 1, 1, 0)  # 1 = whole
# Overwrite with exclusive where present
r_combined <- ifel(r_excl == 1, 2, r_combined)  # 2 = exclusive

# Step 3: Convert to data frame for ggplot
df_combined <- as.data.frame(r_combined, xy = TRUE)
names(df_combined)[3] <- "category"
df_combined$category <- factor(df_combined$category, levels = c(0, 1, 2))

# Step 4: Define colors and labels
combined_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#FFFFFF",   # white
  "1" = "#0090FF",   # light blue = whole extent
  "2" = "#D10000"    # dark blue = exclusive
)

combined_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Overlapping extent",
  "2" = "Exclusive extent"
)

# Step 5: Plot
map_combined_livestock <- ggplot(df_combined, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = category)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = combined_colors,
    breaks = c("1", "2"),    # show only meaningful classes
    labels = combined_labels[c("1", "2")],
    name = "Low conflict opportunity"
  ) +
  geom_sf(data = country_borders, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_sf(data = coastlines, fill = NA, color = "black", size = 0.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  coord_sf(
    xlim = c(2635973, 6525973),
    ylim = c(1448659, 5415659),
    expand = FALSE
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold")
  ) +
  labs(title = "Low livestock-wildlife conflict opportunity (top 80%)")

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, 'opportunities80_livestock_260327.png'),
  plot = map_combined_livestock,
  width = 26, height = 25, units = 'cm', dpi = 300
)

```
